title: T-137 — UI: FA right panel sidebar (fa_right_panel prototype) NPD-a slice of T-020
prompt:
# T-137 — FA right panel slice

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §3, §5 + UX prototypes/design/01-NPD-UX.md fa_right_panel
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:404-452 (fa_right_panel)
Prototype-index entry: fa_right_panel
Parent root: T-020

## Goal
Single Client Component FaRightPanel: a sticky sidebar showing FA summary (code, status, owner, last updated, days_to_launch, Built indicator), quick-action buttons (Dept Close, D365 Build), and a gate progress indicator. Consumes the FA summary prop from parent (RSC); button clicks invoke openModal callback props.

## Implementation contract
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/_components/fa-right-panel.tsx ('use client'). Props: { fa: FaSummary, gateProgress: GateProgress, onOpenModal: (kind: 'deptClose' | 'd365Build', ctx: { fa: FaSummary }) => void }.
- Sticky aside using shadcn Card with: status pill (Badge variant by status), code/name/owner, days_to_launch, Built indicator (Badge), quick-action shadcn Buttons (Dept Close, D365 Build), and a Progress bar for gate progress.
- Button clicks call onOpenModal('deptClose', ...) or onOpenModal('d365Build', ...).
- No DB call inside the component (parent RSC provides fa + gateProgress).

## Files (≤ 2)
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/_components/fa-right-panel.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/[productCode]/_components/__tests__/fa-right-panel.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given FaRightPanel renders with a sample fa + gateProgress, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/fa-screens.jsx:404-452 (fa_right_panel), then it shows status pill + code/name/owner + last_updated + days_to_launch + Built indicator + quick-action buttons + Progress bar using shadcn Card+Badge+Button+Progress primitives — verified by RTL parity checklist
2. Given the user clicks the Dept Close button, when the handler runs, then onOpenModal('deptClose', { fa }) is invoked — verified by RTL
3. Given the user clicks the D365 Build button, when the handler runs, then onOpenModal('d365Build', { fa }) is invoked — verified by RTL

## Test strategy
- RED: fa-right-panel.test.tsx asserting summary structure + Badge variants + button onClick handlers before component exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test fa-right-panel'

## Risk red lines
- Do not call DB or Server Actions from the client component
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim
- Do not exceed scope (1 component + 1 test)
